title: Association of mean corpuscular volume with 28-day mortality in sepsis patients: A retrospective cohort study using eICU data

sample_size.source_quote: A total of 9,415 patients with sepsis were included in the study and the 28-day ICU mortality rate of the sepsis patients was 9.38% (883/9415).

patient_age_mean.source_quote: Our population consisted primarily of older adults (mean age: 65.7±15.8 years), consistent with the demographics of sepsis, where age is a recognized risk factor for adverse outcomes

icu_mortality_percent.source_quote: the 28-day ICU mortality rate of the sepsis patients was 9.38% (883/9415)

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Mean corpuscular volume (MCV) — continuous, per 1 fl increase
mortality_predictors.timing: Within initial 24 hours of ICU admission
mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Multivariate logistic regression (Model II, fully adjusted)
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, Gender, BMI, Temperature, Respiratory rate, Heart rate, MAP, Acute Physiology Score III, APACHE IV score, AIDS, Hepatic failure, Metastatic cancer, Immunosuppression, Albumin, Lactate, Platelets, Hemoglobin, RDW, White blood cell count
mortality_predictors.source_quote: In Model II, which adjusted for all potential confounding variables, the odds ratio for 28-day mortality was 1.11 (95% CI 1.01, 1.23; P = 0.04) for each 1 fl increase in MCV.

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Mean corpuscular volume (MCV) — Q4 (95.5–138.9 fl) vs Q1 (58.0–86.0 fl)
mortality_predictors.timing: Within initial 24 hours of ICU admission
mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Multivariate logistic regression (Model II, fully adjusted)
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, Gender, BMI, Temperature, Respiratory rate, Heart rate, MAP, Acute Physiology Score III, APACHE IV score, AIDS, Hepatic failure, Metastatic cancer, Immunosuppression, Albumin, Lactate, Platelets, Hemoglobin, RDW, White blood cell count
mortality_predictors.source_quote: In the Q4 group (95.5-138.9 fl), the greatest increase in the 28-day risk of death was observed in model II, with an OR of 1.50 (95% CI 1.10, 2.05; P = 0.012).

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Mean corpuscular volume (MCV) — per 1 fl increase, below threshold of 83 fl
mortality_predictors.timing: Within initial 24 hours of ICU admission
mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Two-segment non-linear logistic regression with threshold effect analysis (generalized additive model)
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, Gender, BMI, Temperature, Respiratory rate, Heart rate, MAP, Acute Physiology Score III, APACHE IV score, AIDS, Hepatic failure, Metastatic cancer, Immunosuppression, Albumin, Lactate, Platelets, Hemoglobin, RDW, White blood cell count
mortality_predictors.source_quote: At MCV <83 fl, there was a significant increase in the risk of 28-day mortality with each 1 fl increase in MCV (OR 1.10, 95% CI 1.02, 1.17, P=0.004).

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Mean corpuscular volume (MCV) — per 1 fl increase, above threshold of 83 fl
mortality_predictors.timing: Within initial 24 hours of ICU admission
mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Two-segment non-linear logistic regression with threshold effect analysis (generalized additive model)
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, Gender, BMI, Temperature, Respiratory rate, Heart rate, MAP, Acute Physiology Score III, APACHE IV score, AIDS, Hepatic failure, Metastatic cancer, Immunosuppression, Albumin, Lactate, Platelets, Hemoglobin, RDW, White blood cell count
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The significant positive correlation was absent when MCV was greater than 83fl (OR 1.02, 95% CI 0.99, 1.02, P = 0.31).

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Age (stratified: 15–59 years) — interaction with MCV and 28-day mortality
mortality_predictors.timing: Within initial 24 hours of ICU admission
mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Stratified logistic regression with interaction test
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Stratified analyses showed a significant interaction between age (P = 0.040). The highest risk of 1.04 was found in the age group of 15–59 years (95% CI: 1.02,1.05).
